entry.title: SAP IBP – Automatically Copy Values Within the Same Key Figure for Different Products
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am working with SAP IBP and need to automatically copy values within the same key figure (KF X) from Product A to Product B. The requirement is that this process must be fully automated, meaning no manual adjustments via Excel.

Requirements:

  • Copy values within the same key figure
  • From Product A to Product B
  • No manual intervention (Excel is not an option)
  • No Data Realignment
  • Ideally, the solution should use standard IBP functionalities, such as Forecast Models or other possible approaches

Approaches I have considered so far:

  1. Master Data Rules – Not possible in this case.
  2. Copy Past Periods Forecast Model – Not sure how to configure this approach for my scenario.

I would appreciate any guidance or best practices on how to configure this in IBP. Has anyone successfully implemented a similar requirement?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Hello everyone! We are integrating scripts for SAP Digital Manufacturing.
entry.content:
Hello everyone! We are doing a script integration for SAP Digital Manufacturing, using #POIM and #DRFOUNT. However, it shows the following error in DM:

" 400 Bad Request - {"code":"standardValueTarget.notFound","message":"Standard value targets not found.","correlationId":"81eec77d-ca73-4765-988d-3587f27deb40","details":null}

Can anyone suggest a way?

Thanks!

entry.title: Authentication methods CPI DS Agent
entry.content:
Hi ,

 

I need to use CPI DS agent to set up integration between on prem system and IBP . What all authentication methods does CPI DS agent support for its connectivity to on prem . I want to avoid maintaining the credential on DS  on the cloud .. Can we have a oAuth connection between the DS Agent in the DMZ and on prem system ?Is there any documentation that I could follow for this scenario ?

entry.title: Re: SAP Change Document(CD): Special Scenarios with SOLMAN (Solution Manager)
entry.content:
@Dave_S : In large organizations with multiple teams, such as Project A (Phoenix) and Project B (Helux), situations may arise where both teams need to work on the same object, with priority assigned to one team over the other. In such cases, segregation is essential to prevent conflicts and ensure smooth development processes

Reversing changes is necessary when a modification is no longer required. However, until these changes are fully reverted, other teams may be unable to access or modify the affected object due to existing dependencies.

 

While CSOL is a valuable feature for preventing simultaneous modifications and potential conflicts, there are exceptional situations where manual intervention is required. In such special scenarios, it may be necessary to bypass/remove CSOL.

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager GIS
entry.content:
Hi Experts! 

 

I have a question regarding the SAM x GIS integration. i understand that the feature layers is created in GIS and appears on SAM. But is there some integration of SAP x GIS that we input the coordinates in GIS of some equipments that already are created in SAP but without location, and we can visualize in SAM Map the equipment? 

Or the integration SAM and GIS is just for the feature layers and for SAP business Object we can just create in SAM Map?

 

Best Regard,

 

Rafael Eij Umeda

SAP Service and Asset Manager

entry.title: Re: Attach excel xlsx file to email using cl_bcs and send to MS Outlook recipient
entry.content:
Do we have solution for this? I am facing the same issue since we have some changes in M365 outlooks. My SOST shows correct filename, but outlooks show the header as my filename instead of subject. I am using CL_Document_BCS.

In example BCS_EXAMPLE_7 - header is not passed to the method. When I test this program it shows the SOST and Outlook attachment with same name. I have commented the header( i_attachment_header ) line in my Add attachment method. Now my SOST and Outlook file shows the same name.

entry.title: Hosting a BusinessOne webclient extension with a PHP backend in BTP
entry.content:
Hello all,

We have developed a vertical add-on for SAP BusinessOne that has a Fiori UI5 frontend and a PHP backend (using Laravel) and we're looking into adapting it to run as a Webclient Extension hosted on the BTP.

We have been reviewing documentation, courses, tutorials, and sample projects and while we mostly understand the process and the steps outlined, we’ve noticed that they largely assume the use of SAP Business Application Studio (or SAP Build Code) and/or a Node.js backend, often incorporating the SAP CAP programming model.

However, we have invested countless hours into developing several vertical add-ons in PHP, so a full conversion from PHP to Node.js is not a feasible option for us. Ideally, we’d like to keep most of our existing PHP backend.

Our main question is whether keeping our existing PHP services is a realistic approach. If so, we would greatly appreciate any relevant documentation - whether in the form of a course, sample project, tutorial, or any other resource - that could guide us in the right direction.

A couple of examples of our known challenges right now are:

1. Running our php service in the Cloud Foundry environment, as the php build pack doesn't include some extensions that are required by the laravel framework (such as OpenSSL).

2. Implementing SSO (or even principal propagation). We have sucessfully made requests to the ServiceLayer straight from the frontend because the webclient provides a SESSION cookie, but we need to be able to perform these requests in our backend, reusing the Service Layer session from the webclient so that the requests are done with the same user that logged on to the webclient.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Method 'CREATE_STREAM' not implemented in data provid...
entry.content:
Hello again i was able to use the file upload feature only on Odata v4 and i did it by following the below links

Start by following this blog for implemting the file upload feature

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/streams-in-rap-uploading-pdf-excel-and-other-files-in-rap-application/ba-p/13526891

In OData V4 we have to enable the draft or we will not be able to view the create button, so enable the draft by following this blog

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/understanding-and-using-draft-tables-in-sap-s-rap-model/ba-p/13882473

After applying everything try and check if it work, if it doesn't and you face this error 
Virus scan profile /IWBEP/V4/ODATA_UPLOAD is not active
Refer to this Note 3010735 - F4111 - Virus scan profile /IWBEP/V4/ODATA_UPLOAD is not active

 

Best of luck everyone.

entry.title: Define Priorities in Maintenance Management, S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm implementing SAP S/4HANA Asset Management, Public Cloud Edition.

In the different configuration options available through Central Business Configuration, there is one called 'Define Priorities for Each Priority Type.':

What I need to know is whether there is a configuration point where each Priority Type is associated with a Notification Type and an Order Type, given that both notifications and orders have date fields. If not, do I have to understand that the Priority Type Y1 is always assigned to both the Notification Type Y1 and the Order Type YA01? In this case, when is the system intended to use the Priority Type G0?

Additionally, I understand that new Priority Types cannot be defined and that only the standard ones provided by the system can be used.

Grateful for your reply in advance.

entry.title: Re: Copy Values within same KF with different Product ID
entry.content: Hi , Thanks for your input! I see your point about using attribute transformation with a custom MDT to store the "From → To" relationship for product mapping. However, in this case, I don’t think this approach is suitable for copying key figure values within the same key figure for a different Product ID. Why Attribute Transformation is not the right solution here: 🔹 Attribute Transformations are designed for master data, not transactional key figure values. They work well when modifying attributes (e.g., assigning categories or flags) but don’t perform direct key figure value copies. 🔹 Using a custom MDT with a reference ID does not solve the issue of copying values within the same key figure. The planning area still expects the key figure values to be assigned at the original product level, and additional logic would be needed to ensure correct mapping. 🔹 Cyclic dependencies and planning area activation issues. As you mentioned, if KF1 is used as a source for KF2, we can’t use KF2 as a source to update back KF1. This further complicates the process and introduces unnecessary complexity. Why Realignment Makes More Sense If the requirement is ad-hoc, then realignment rules are the best option. They are designed to reassign key figure values based on a mapping table and work within the same key figure without needing additional logic. Would you agree that realignment is the best approach for this use case, or do you see another potential method that aligns better with SAP IBP's capabilities? Thanks for your insights! Best regards, Kacper

entry.title: Re: Native driver not recognised
entry.content:
Every Windows Server comes with a basic SQL SERVER client driver suitable for ODBC connections.

For SAP you need to install this driver: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=56041

Native client is no longer distributed with the SQL SERVER.

entry.title: Re: Intrastat not showing automatic CN
entry.content: Thank you Hakan,

entry.title: Re: Difference in SAP Integration Suite for SAP SuccessFactors Solution Suite std edition
entry.content: Hi, I have a question: what the difference in integration with "other systems" and "external system"?By other systems do you mean other SAP systems (e.g. S/4)?

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori catalog not loading
entry.content: That is weird but please notice the following. You must select a service first to check the ICF nodes section. Please try again by selecting the the INTEROP service. However the other errors in STC01 might indicate some inconsistency or lack of authorization in SICF. By the way, when seaching nodes in SICF do not use the "Technical Service Name". Instead use the "External service name". All services in /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE are under the path /sap/opu/odata so you can inspect them manually without any filters by manually expanding the tree.

entry.title: Re: Calculating Energy Consumption in the Product Carbon Footprint
entry.content:
Hi User931,

In our demo we also did what Klaus described and use Internal Service Confirmations (Coming from Activities in Production Order Confirmations) mapped to a resource via an Energy Flow Model to assign gas usage to the PCF, see this example.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori catalog not loading
entry.content: The same thing for when registering services. You asked about the INTEROP service but are trying to register something called ZCA_CO* - that's is not the same thing

entry.title: Re: Intrastat not showing automatic CN
entry.content: Thank you Hakan, but the problem exists. as reporting those credit notes is legal requirement not only in Latvia but for example also in Germany. So basically it would mean that SAP Intrastat dispatches report is not in line with legal requirement.

entry.title: Re: Transition from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM...
entry.content: This post is not a question.  Inserting 'answer' here to remove from unanswered questions listing.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025
entry.content: Thx for the fast reply and actions. Everything looking good now. Was able to update my 4.3 installation with BI2025 and it worked although I have it running on windows server 2019 which doesn't seem to a be supported OS but ok, can't have everything.

entry.title: MM_PUR_S4_PR~MODIFY_ITEM Contract for limit missing in importing parameters
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am implementing a BAdI enhancement for Purchase Requisitions (PRs) and need to update the Fixed Supplier field with the supplier from the Contract for Limit.

For the SAP GUI, I have implemented the logic in IF_EX_ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST~PROCESS_ITEM, and it works correctly in ME51N—the Fixed Supplier field is properly populated.

However, I am facing an issue in the Manage Purchase Requisition Professional (F2229) app. While the BAdI executes when creating a PR in this app, the Fixed Supplier field is not immediately updated in the UI. The correct value appears only after navigating back to the PR header and then returning to the item level. It seems that the update is not reflected in the draft mode in real time.

I explored using MM_PUR_S4_PR~MODIFY_ITEM to handle this, but the Contract for Limit field is not available in the importing structure (as shown in the attached image).

Could you please advise on how to resolve this? I feel like I’ve searched everywhere for a solution without success.

I would greatly appreciate any guidance, suggestions, or alternative approaches.

Thank you very much for your help!

Best regards,
Veronika

entry.title: Report for unsubmitted time sheets
entry.content:
Hello, I need a report that shows me all of the unsubmitted time sheets for a specific date range. Is there a way to do this?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Re: PGR not happening while doing the sale return
entry.content:
First you need to convert the special sale order stock to unrestricted for which, if you try to create a return delivery against a return order, system will throw an error saying ""Special Stock E not supported""

For that, in OVZI, you have to change the settings for the item category in such a way that for REN, 1 should be assigned under the tab "Origin / Source Requirement Type". Once you do this, system will allow you to do PGR and take the stocks to either quality or unrestricted depending upon Business needs.

entry.title: Re: SAP Profile Generation "Merge" function
entry.content:
Hello there,

We`re having the same issue. Have you found a solution in the meantime?

Thank you,

Mihai

entry.title: Re: US Payroll - PSD code selection (payroll & W2)
entry.content:
The main program that evaluates the payroll results for US Tax Reporter is RPCTRCU0.  I see some Pennsylvania specific routines like process_wages_pa_act_32 where the program comments also say...

* The Pennsylvania Act 32 requires that wages in W-2 be reported
* to the locality to where taxes are remitted. This routine is
* responsible for cumulating the payroll results based on the

Is it that you believe the amount should not have been taken?  Not reported? or reported in a different location on the W-2.  It could be that you need to back up a step and look at how the payroll result was calculated.

The tax form configuration is under IMG step Payroll: USA -> Tax Reporter -> Define original tax forms and/or Tax Form Groups.  The technical name of the W-2 is HR_F_W2.  Income tax (type 01) is usually reported with Tax Form Field T001.

entry.title: Re: Optimising Warehouse Automation: SAP EWM Integration with WCS/WES
entry.content: Very useful and nicely articulated.

entry.title: Re: LV61A081 LV61A082 - pricing date vs billing date
entry.content:
SAP note 1256361 should address this issue for sure.  In case, you still have an issue, ensure, the routines 82 & 333 are set as per SAP note 2555008. 

With all these settings, still, it is not working, then, before going for any custom validation, raise a ticket with SAP by referring note 1256361 has not solved your issue.  They will guide you

entry.title: Re: Export S4 hana data as a file to GCS using datasphere
entry.content:
Of course - you can use Replication Flows to do so.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/aec242c29188408c9ebe1a3ab63ce28b.html#loioaec242c29188408c9ebe1a3ab63ce28b__GCS_usage

Bear in mind that this involves Premium Outbound Integration costs!

entry.title: Parallel crcy area (crcy type 30) missing for area 01 ch. of deprec. 1000Message No. ACC_AA116
entry.content:
Working in an S/4'HANA on prem version 2023. When trying to maintain Company Code in Asset Accounting I get the message ACC_AA116. I checked several times the Customizing of GL and AA but could not find the currency settings for crcy type 30 for area 01. I have checked knowledge base article 2307443 but couldn't find the error.
Transaction FINSC_LEDGER looks like screenshot "finsc_ledger_glob_curr", 
"curr_conversion" and "company code settings for ledger". 
Can anybody give me a hint to solve this problem?
Kind regards

entry.title: Re: CIS (Cloud Identity Services) - IAS & IPS
entry.content: Please use abbreviation SCI, not CIS. At least in the BTP context, CIS usually means "commercial infrastructure services", i.e. BTP account management.

entry.title: How to transport recurrent calendar Tasks between 2 different SAC Tenants
entry.content:
Some SAC customers asked me if it would be possible to transport recurrent calendar tasks (like data/multi actions tasks) cross tenants.

With the Q4.2024 SAC release (Plan Process Management - template support) it is possible but there are some steps that need to be followed in order to do that.

First you need to create a process on the Source Tenant and include the Data/Multi Action tasks within that (You will need to remove the recurrence to do that):

After creating the process, please create any general task with the left checkbox for the process selected:

You can select the Context Model for the task

Select the process again and now click on Save as Template

Save it on the folder that you are planning to transport (where the model and other objects are located)

Now it can be selected as an object for export within the transport menu

Proceed with the transport as usual and you will see the template saved on the target tenant:

Open the Process Template and click on Create Process from Template:

Now you should have a process with the calendar tasks on your calendar:

If you need to put recurrence on the calendar task you should remove the parent dependency from it:

OR

Now the Add Recurrence button should be enabled:

Configure the recurrence as required and update the task

The generated process can be deleted in case you don’t need it anymore:

Go to the Series Menu and rename it accordingly

Now you should have your Calendar Tasks transported properly.
For more information on this topic please check Work with Calendar Templates - SAC Help page

entry.title: Re: is there a way to maintain or upload custom css files...
entry.content:
Hi Mahesh,

The only way to achieve this level of control would be through a custom widget, allowing you to inject css directl to the platform. This could be risky as it may impact other UI elements so you would want to be careful with this approach. Below I have provided sample code for a custom widget that would enable this:

cssinject.json:

{
	"id": "com.sap.sample.cssinject",
	"version": "1.0.0",
	"name": "CSSInject",
	"description": "Inject custom CSS into the page",
	"newInstancePrefix": "CSSInject",
	"icon": "/cssinject.png",
	"vendor": "SAP",
	"eula": "",
	"license": "",
	"webcomponents": [
		{
			"kind": "main",
			"tag": "com-sap-sample-cssinject",
			"url": "/cssinject.js",
			"integrity": "sha256-Ltwh39JeN5eaRtv2+Dt1r7dOgaG4CSiAJekTwHARm9A=",
			"ignoreIntegrity": false
		},
		{
			"kind": "builder",
			"tag": "com-sap-sample-cssinject-builder",
			"url": "/cssinject_builder.js",
			"integrity": "sha256-Q4vxnL/nIKeKPbqECKpc8chOlN6rYKjQvN9INBdcezI=",
			"ignoreIntegrity": false
		}
	],
	"properties": {
		"customCSS": {
			"type": "string",
			"description": "Custom CSS to apply",
			"default": ""
		}
	},
	"methods": {
		"setCustomCSS": {
			"description": "Sets the custom CSS",
			"parameters": [
				{
					"name": "css",
					"type": "string",
					"description": "The new CSS to apply"
				}
			]
		},
		"getCustomCSS": {
			"returnType": "string",
			"description": "Returns the currently set custom CSS"
		}
	},
	"events": {
		"onCSSChange": {
			"description": "Triggered when custom CSS is updated."
		}
	}
}

Then the supporting files are in the archive I have hosted on GitHub: https://icamac.github.io/SAC/customwidgets/cssinject/Archive.zip

Once you have installed the custom widget on your SAC tenant, you will see a new custom widget. Adding this widget to your story (the widget itself is invisible) will allow you to open the builder panel, where you can enter any custom css for your story:

I hope this is useful.

Cheers, Ivan.

entry.title: Re: Setup Synthetic user Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM with On-Prem Runner
entry.content:
Hi @Madhuviswanath,
Since it is also possible for different 3rd party cloud providers, I suppose in theory it should also work with different on-prem webdrivers. However that would need some try-and-error work.

entry.title: impex and default value for usergroup
entry.content:
I want create an impex that create a set of users.

My issue is on set the default value for the attribute groups

This is the header:

UPDATE Employee ; uid[unique = true]; name ; groups(uid)[default = myUserGroup][mode=append] ; password[default = $defaultPassword] ; sessionLanguage(isocode)[default=$defaultLanguage] ; sessionCurrency(isocode)[default = $defaultCurrency]

 the predicate [default = myUserGroup][mode=append] don't have any effect.

  • myUserGroup exists,
  • i'm using the append mode
  • others default works fine

If I wrote the groups line by line it works fine.

What's wrong?

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA ML in India 2025-03 Recap
entry.content:
At the beginning of March 2025, as part of the ML/AI Tour my fellow dev advocate @noravonthenen and I did in India, we held five SAP CodeJam events in Kolkata, Pune, and Bengaluru.

Two of these events focused on getting started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python.

Equally important were chances to catch up with some organizers and participants in informal post-work settings...

...or with our SAP colleagues.

I am glad Nora and I could help so many participants to go through the journey of improving their skills working with ML and AI!

Traditionally, I very much enjoyed SAP Stammtisch at the end of the last CodeJam of the Tour.

#IamSAP 

I always take the opportunity to experience at least a little bit of the city I am in, so you can see my photos:

  • Kolkata: https://www.instagram.com/p/DG1wL0OydCp/ 
  • Pune: https://www.instagram.com/p/DG75rf0MPkt/ 

Now, the next stop for me is in Kraków on April 3rd.

Would you like to host such an SAP CodeJam? Here are the details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/quot-getting-started-with-machine-learning-usin...

entry.title: Re: SAP Change Document(CD): Special Scenarios with SOLMAN (Solution Manager)
entry.content: @Surendra_Karma Thanks. I appreciate understanding the circumstances better.

entry.title: Re: SAP Profile Generation "Merge" function
entry.content:
Talking with SAP Support Developer they said it was "working as designed" even though it did not do this in any of our legacy netweaver systems.

 

What I ended up doing after a number of different tests was to just completely remove the Tcode(s) that were having the issue, saving the role, and then adding them back and then resetting the correct values based on comparing the role to a version of it in QA/SBX.

I did figure out that this only affected the roles that I exported from our legacy Netweaver system and then imported into the new S/4 HANA system, so it looks like something goofy happens at the table association level when you transfer roles between these 2 types of systems via the Upload/Download process.

I realized that this was NOT happening with any new roles that I manually created and then later updated directly in the new S/4 HANA system.

 

It would be nice if there was a Security migration tool for S/4 upgrades, because having to move all of your legacy security like Roles,  and SUGR, Table, Program Auth groups manually is a very time consuming process.     

Especially if you have custom Tcodes that were not set up in the new system as that causes the role upload to completely fail.

entry.title: Re: SDI MAX DB Adapter for DP agent
entry.content: Does any one created Max DB adapter? If possible can you share the details please

entry.title: Re: Visualizing Your SAP Bill of Materials Using CAP, Fiori elements, and the UI5 Network Graph

entry.title: Unlock the expertise of top SAP professionals through live sessions - Register now!
entry.content:
With your SAP Learning Hub subscription, you can take advantage of expert-led live sessions, to dive deeper into focus topics, ask questions and maximize your skills


SAP Business Technology Platform

  • Basic Trial for Enterprise Automation with SAP
     March 20, 11:00 - 12:30 (CET)
  • Getting Started with the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine
    March 27, 16:00 - 17:00 (CET)

Artificial Intelligence (AI)

  • SAP Business Technology Platform Pillars - AI Solutions
    March 14, 09:00 - 11:00 (CET) or from 16:00 - 18:00 (CET)
  • Managing the Full Lifecycle of AI Models on SAP Business Technology Platform
    March 31, 08:00 - 10:00 (CET) or from 16:00 - 18:00 (CET)

Enterprise Management

  • SAP S/4HANA Sales: Automatic Data Determination (Plant/Shipping/Route)
    March 19, 16:00 - 17:00 (CET)
  • Business Partner Master Data in SAP S/4HANA from the Purchasing Perspective
     March 20, 15:00 - 17:00 (CET)

As an SAP Learning Hub subscriber, you can join this live session to explore the various methods of data integration, with a focus on the best options for different scenarios. You will learn how to implement various data integration environments, ranging from a simple file import to an enterprise level data load. If you are involved in an incentive management implementation of any size, you don’t want to miss this session. Read more

Please note
You can find the full list of live sessions here and register directly with your SAP Learning Hub subscription.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Trial Account error
entry.content:
It sounds like you have a basic trial.

I would recommend writing to sapbasictrial@sap.com and describing the issues in the email.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: SAP TM ERP Document Flow Navigation Through Fiori
entry.content: Thank you @Rogerio_Lima  for sharing. Also, I could notice this new screen opens always in IE not in MS Edge. Is there any hack to fix that?

entry.title: Re: Unexpected Daily Backup in HANA DB – Where Else Could...
entry.content:
If backups are still running despite disabling schedules in HANA Cockpit, DB13, and crontabs, so please check the following:

- HANA Database Configuration (global.ini) – Look for backup settings under [backup] in global.ini.
- HANA SQL Backup Schedules – Run:
SELECT * FROM M_BACKUP_CATALOG WHERE ENTRY_TYPE_NAME = 'data backup';
This might reveal if backups are triggered by an internal job.
- SAP Basis Jobs (SM37) – Check if any background job in SAP triggers backups.
- 3rd-Party Backup Tools – Some external backup tools (e.g., NetBackup, Commvault) may be triggering backups outside SAP.

If none of these help, enable audit logging to trace backup triggers.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Transportation Route Proposal in Export Do...
entry.content: The transportation route in export documents is determined based on standard SAP logic, which considers only the departure and ultimate consignee’s country. Since BAdI /SAPSLL/BADI_CDOC_PROP_N does not support route modifications, may you can try the to check Route Determination Settings (OVT0, OVR3) and ensure the consignee’s country is included in the calculation. Use a Custom Enhancement in BAdI /SAPSLL/BADI_TRNSP_ROUTE if available and modify Route Manually as a workaround if automation is not feasible.

entry.title: Re: How to set rounding for picking only a full HU or bag...
entry.content:
Hi Johannes! Thanks for your help on the rounding part of it. When I do that, the wt is to pick 1 kg, even when the required quantity is less.
But I need the wt to be for a whole bag (which has 5kg), not just for 1kg. Any ideia on how to do that?

entry.title: Re: RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition : Secure Cloud Connectivity
entry.content:
Great effort, thanks for the deep dive 🎉

When it comes to VPC Peering you gave a link to AWS' VPC peering but did not discuss it in depth as Azure or GPC. Is there a reason for this (like AWS does not support a complete feauture set in this area when compared to e.g. Azure) or was it just so "same same" with Azure that a duplication would not have brought any new insights?

Thanks again and cheers

Jens

entry.title: Re: Not able to login to ABAP ADT
entry.content:
This issue happens normaly, when authentication tokens are not synced properly.

You can try following:

- Clear Browser Cache & Cookies and retry.
- Use "Sign in with SAP Universal ID" instead of entering credentials manually.
- Check SAP BTP Cockpit – Ensure your trial account is active.
- Reset Password if login fails repeatedly.
- Try a Different Network or VPN in case of firewall restrictions.

If the issue persists, please open a support ticket.

entry.title: Re: Upgrade from R/3 to S/4 - Payroll and Time advices
entry.content: Upgrading R/3 Payroll and Time to S/4HANA requires careful planning. Ensure compatibility with HCM for S/4HANA on-prem or consider migrating to SAP SuccessFactors. You should test all payroll schemas, time evaluations, and reports before go-live. For existing R/3 to SF integrations, check middleware (e.g., SAP CPI) and update APIs accordingly. A phased approach with thorough testing ensures a smooth transition.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite – Edge Integration Cell on Azure
entry.content: The blog is certainly much appreciated and the use cases are well described. But I have a query related to logs and traces and not sure where they will be stored in case of any error messages. Does it store these logs at On premises or at parent cloud location. Appreciate your response here.

entry.title: Re: How to set rounding for picking only a full HU or bag...
entry.content:
Hi,

difficult to say without seeing all the data. Have you setup the quantity classification?

Please check the documentation:

Round Warehouse Task Quantity to Whole Units

Rounding warehouse tasks (WTs) to whole units.

Use

You can configure how the WT quantity should be rounded. You can do this at storage type level and in the warehouse process type.

You can set up the following rounding mechanisms:

  • Round down to a single unit
  • Round down to a multiple of a unit
  • Round up to a multiple of a unit.
  • Round to the available quantity in the source bin

The rounding logic is based on the following data:

  • A storage type can have an assigned quantity classifier for the warehouse, which would then be valid for all products in this storage type.
  • For each product, you can assign a quantity classifier for the warehouse in the product master, which then overrides the setting described above.

Process: If a WT was created and you have switched on rounding, the system checks the quantity classifier for the warehouse at storage type level, and in the product master. The system searches for a match in the configuration activity Define Unit of Measure Attributes and rounds the WT quantity according to the unit of measure that is maintained there.

 

 

Example

 

 

In bulk storage, you do not normally want to manage partial pallets. Partial pallets should be picked from a special partial pallet storage area. The following example should clarify the effects this switch can have on system behavior.

The requested quantity is 2.5 pallets. The quantity in the bin is 5 pallets:

  • Rounding up:
    Causes a removal of 3 pallets
  • Round down to a multiple:
    Causes a removal of 2 pallets. For the remaining half pallet, the system determines suitable stock using the storage type search.
  • Round down to a single unit:
    Causes a removal of 1 pallet. For the remaining 1.5 pallets, the system determines suitable stock using the storage type search. The system finds the bulk storage area again, and another 1 pallet is removed using a second warehouse task. For the remaining half pallet, the system determines suitable stock using the storage type search. The attribute is used to avoid partial pallets, and to create individual warehouse tasks for each pallet during stock removal.

entry.title: Re: Node Functions in SAP CPI for Data Transformation.
entry.content:

Nice blog regarding the node functions.

entry.title: Re: ASE 16.0 future patch releases
entry.content: Will there be a new patch SP05 for ASE 16.0?

entry.title: Re: AIF - AIF Error and Monitoring doesn't display any warning message
entry.content: Hello, to be able to give you suggestions on where the error is more information is required. For example: how does your AIF interface look like, are recipients assigned,... can you give more details about what you have done?

entry.title: Re: 2H 2024: Latest Updates to the Reimagined Applicant Management Tool in SAP SuccessFactors Recrui
entry.content:
Please confirm that the Legacy Applicant tool will NOT be deprecated in 1H2025 due to the new enhancements that will be released with 1H2025 and 2H2025.

Is this correct based on the information stated below?  Can we still opt-out after 1H2025 release?

Upcoming release timeline for the redesigned Applicant Management tool and depreciation of the Legacy Workbench:

  • 2H 2024 Release: Currently, each Recruiting User can select to view either the Legacy or Redesigned Applicant Management Tool with a toggle in the tool. For customers who only want use the new Applicant Management tool can remove the Legacy Workbench in Admin Settings. Customers who do not want to allow for the Redesigned Applicant Management tool, Admin Opt-Out is available within Admin Settings. 
  • 1H 2025 and 2H 2025 Releases: New capabilities and enhancements delivered, ensuring key functionality is available in the Redesigned Applicant Management tool. 
  • 1H 2026 Release: Legacy Workbench is not available. Universal Push for all customers. 
  • 2H 2026: Full Depreciation of the Legacy Workbench. 

thank you.

Kim

entry.title: Re: Can I export and import a calender task between two SAC tenants
entry.content: It is possible now. Please check https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-transport-recurrent-calendar-tasks-between-2-different-sac-tenants/ba-p/14044966

entry.title: Supplier shipping performance report
entry.content:
Hello MM experts,

I need to know is there any standard report available to measure the vendor shipping performance report based on MM Schedule agreement schedule line Versus Vendor shipping notification ( ASN).

I know any vendor performance report in SAP work based on GR data but we need vendor performance from ASN . Table we need to discuss here is EKET and EKES.

Or if any of you have any option n for shipping performance report let me know.

This is for ECC 6 system

 

Gururajan A

entry.title: Re: Cascading Budget and Planner Hierarchy
entry.content:
Hi @pbrandi 

Yes Budget Cascading works with Second Manager hierarchy.

I am very bias against this feature and try to always stir customers and consultants from using it (I've implemented it once for a bank in Europe). I know some companies like it but it defeats the purpose of "Reward to Retain" because lower levels of the orgchart potentially won't get anything just because higher levels took everything. There is no way to set a max for how much each level takes from the budget and if money runs out 3 levels before the bottom of the orgchart the planners for each of these three levels don't get any funding to increase their teams. 

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: How to Post Recurring Journal Entry Voucher Automatically? I setup that but J/E is not posted.
entry.content: Hello, what value did you set for due date calculation? If you navigate to General Ledger > Post Recurring Journal Entry Vouchers and select the run you can review the log history below. It generally has to do with the date on the Recurring JEV and the date on the posting run. Things to consider: Are the dates on the recurring JEVs all consistent? Or do they vary?

When will CR2025 be available to try/purchase?

Thank you,

Roberto

entry.title: Re: Cascading Budget and Planner Hierarchy
entry.content: Thanks Xavier, Much appreciated on resonding. Sorry that I may not have been clear, can the Rollup work? Agreed but the client has a unique way of budgeting and this is the only way it can be done.

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part II: Provisioning to BTP and S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello @MartinRaepple ,

The SCIM API that is used to connect Entra ID with SAP CIS is already deprecated:
https://api.sap.com/api/IAS_SCIM/ 

I tried to use its successor (https://api.sap.com/api/IdDS_SCIM), however you get an error because the namespace urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User is not working. 

For a new implementation I don't have a good feeling to use the deprecated version of the API. Do you know if/when the new version will be supported? 

Best regards,
Markus

Thanks & Regards,

Bhaskar Nagula

entry.content: You should post OABT screen

entry.title: User-Exit for BOM creation with Copy-from Button
entry.content:
Hi SAP gurus,

In my business scenario I need to update a material characteristic (in its Material Master Classification view) by doing some calculus on some of its BOM component quantities.

Since quantities can be edited in creation mode and change mode, I need to check components handling quantities in both sides.

When using CS01 (or CS02) I have no problem on using BAdI BOM_UPDATE and method CHECK_AT_SAVE to achieve this, and it works.

When it comes to use CS01 but with Copy-from button, wich creates the BOM starting from another material BOM, none of the User-Exit found in the community works.
I've deeply searched here and also tried all of the PCSD00* User-exit, placing break-points in any Includes, but it doesn't work.

Again, I've also tried checking the other Methods CHANGE_IN_UPDATE and CHANGE_BEFORE_UPDATE with no results.

 

Does anyone has suggestions on this ?
Thanks

entry.title: Variable Pay - Exclusion of employees with Performance Rating "N/A" and Individual Payout % "blank"
entry.content:
Dear community, 
An issue has been detected during the launch of worksheet for employees who have the PM forms not completed (Only use completed forms is flagged). In details:
- PM form not completed and Ind Rating in worksheet N/A
- Ind Percent not filled in 
- Final Payout calculated as Ind Calc Amount

Is there a way to exclude employees with a Ind Rating < 50%?

In attachment a screenshot. 

Thank you 

SAP SuccessFactors Compensation

entry.title: Best practice: how to load data in CFIN for NON SAP acquisitions
entry.content:
Hi all,

As I'm looking to manage new acquisitions in Group Reporting, I have heard that SAP suggests loading the data of the new companies directly in Central Finance. I would like to know if there a guide or a best practice regarding the load of the full trail balance of the NON SAP ,to be acquired, companies directly in CFIN (only then proceed to data uploads from the ACDOCA table into the ACDOCU table via Release universal journal to have the consolidation of investments)?

Thanks in advance for any insights and suggestions.

Knowing that all permissions have been checked RBP for external User and the PFS link and password worked as expected and i can upload document in Maintain Onboarding and Offboarding Document Templates.

So, I "d like to know if you have any idea about this issue?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Using HR_MAINTAIN_MASTERDATA
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am using FM HR_MAINTAIN_MASTERDATA to update numerous infotypes, one record at a time.  When I check the return parameter, no error message is given, but when I try to COMMIT WORK AND WAIT, it aborts, even though I have the COMMIT statement wrapped inside a TRY/CATCH block.  The error implies that it violates a time constraint rule, but to me, the data looks fine.

My question is why doesn't the call to HR_MAINTAIN_MASTERDATA return the error message?

entry.title: Threaded mode SAP ASE 16 and Linux Hyper/Multi threaded cpus(cores)
entry.content:
Good Day, 

Reading sap kba 2450359 - 'How to configure number of thread when using threaded kernel mode? - ASE ,

it's content mentions:

'For an ASE using 'threaded' kernel mode, the recommendation is for the number of threads
to be configured as follows: Number of Threads = (Number of CPUs - 2).

For example: 16 CPUs on the host, the number of thread should be set to 14.'

This example assumes a 1-1 relationship , i.e, 1 core  spans 1 OS thread?

So if we have the same 16 cores(cpus) in which each core spans 2 threads, then we'll have 32 logical processors. In this case syb_default_pool should tune to 30?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Supplier shipping performance report
entry.content:
As far I am aware--There is no standard report on Shipping performance against the ASN. SAP does standard performance based on time delivery. You are right--you have to consider EKET to know the delivery date --EKES for ASN information and maybe EKBE to know the actual delivery date and then come up with the information or report you are trying to build.

if you are using S4-- there is a predictive delivery date that is applicable for purchase orders but i think your question is related to the Scheduling agreement.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/af9ef57f504840d2b81be8667206d485/69908735733a41eeb84bc61d9d0e4208.html

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-feature/e0dd79ff-4ed9-4d79-9461-2be67e664a3c/

entry.title: Unlocking the Future of Sales & Service Business Processes with SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2
entry.content:
I am happy to share that we are bringing you a second series of webcasts in the context of SAP Sales and Service Cloud Modernization

Our upcoming webcast series explores the game-changing potential of SAP Sales and Service Cloud version 2, diving deep into everything required to embrace intelligent & automated processes. We will guide you through key topics, including:

  • Mindset shifts for automation-driven processes, such as transitioning from Opportunity Management to Guided Selling, and from Ticket Handling to Case Management.
  • The Side-by-Side Approach, leveraging SAP BTP for extensibility and seamless integrations.
  • New development paradigms, from Low-Code/No-Code solutions to Pro-Code customization.
  • Integration best practices with your backend system, also including CTI (with Sinch) and partner solutions like Mediafly.

Each session comes packed with valuable information, practical guidance, and when applicable, demos from seasoned SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Experts.

Join us to explore your future with SAP Sales and Service Cloud version 2 and ensure your business & IT are prepared for the next wave of innovation!

 To attend the series and ask questions from the Q&A panel, click the session links to register (or for the replays)!

Webcast 9: Working with Guided Selling in SAP Sales Cloud version 2: How to prepare your organization

  • Date:  22nd of April 2025 @ 4pm CET 
  • Target Audience: All ‘SAP Cloud for Customer’ customers 
  • Speakers: Hendrik Gerdes (Customer Value Adviser), Grayson Gates (Solution Value Advisor SAP CX)

Webcast 10: An example of CTI integration in Sales and Service Cloud version 2 with Sinch

  • Date:  6th of May 2025 @ 4pm CET 
  • Target Audience: All SAP Sales and Service Cloud customers 
  • Speakers: Andrea Mazzanti (SAP CX Expert Service), Jonathan Ingram (Sinch Product Engineer)

Webcast 11: Working with Case Management in SAP Service Cloud version 2: How to prepare your organization

  • Date:  13rd of May 2025 @ 4pm CET 
  • Target Audience: All ‘SAP Cloud for Customer’ customers 
  • Speakers: Marcus Echter (Customer Value Adviser), Brian Kyser (Solution Value Advisor SAP CX)

Webcast 12: :Leverage the In App extensibility features in Sales and Service Cloud version 2

  • Date:  10th of June 2025 @ 4pm CET 
  • Target Audience: All SAP Sales and Service Cloud customers 
  • Speakers: Jyoti Teotia (SAP Sales and Service Product Manager), Salvatore D'Urso (SAP CX Expert Service)

Webcast 13:How to set up SAP BTP to be prepared for extensions in Sales and Service Cloud

  • Date:  24th of June 2025 @ 4pm CET 
  • Target Audience: All SAP Sales and Service Cloud customers 
  • Speakers: Markus Mehlich (SAP CX Expert Service), Arulraj Athiseharan (SAP CX Expert Service)

Webcast 14: Integrate a partner solution in Sales and Service Cloud version 2 - Example with Mediafly 

  • Date:  16th of September 2025 @ 4pm CET 
  • Target Audience: All SAP Sales and Service Cloud customers 
  • Speakers: Salvatore D'Urso (SAP CX Expert Service), Ethan Berceli (Mediafly product engineer)

Webcast 15: Extensions with "Low Code No Code" in Sales and Service Cloud version 2

  • Date:  30th of September 2025 @ 4pm CET 
  • Target Audience: All SAP Sales and Service Cloud customers 
  • Speakers: Markus Kufner (Solution Value Advisor)  

Webcast 16: New Integration paradigms in Sales and Service Cloud version 2

  • Date:  14th of October 2025 @ 4pm CET 
  • Target Audience: All SAP Sales and Service Cloud customers 
  • Speakers: Markus Mehlich (SAP CX Expert Service), Timo John (Solution Value Advisor SAP BTP)

Webcast 17: An example of extension with Pro code example & Custom Services in SAP Sales and Service Cloud version 2

  • Date:  14th of October 2025 @ 4pm CET 
  • Target Audience: All SAP Sales and Service Cloud customers 
  • Speakers: Jyoti Teotia (SAP Sales and Service Product Manager), Geetha A.R. (SAP Sales and Service Product Manager)

entry.title: SAP FSCM Credit management - web services configuration without PI Issue
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have done some SAP FSCM Credit management - web services configuration without PI. The setup is configured as a local shortcut back to same ECC system.

 

New FSCM setups config done with the help of below Link-:

https://vbalko.blogspot.com/2020/01/sap-fscm-credit-management-web-services.html?m=1

Now with the updated FSCM config, post-processing is done via a real user Authorization , whereas in the old PI config setup, the same post-processing was done via system ID

Kindly advise how we can add the technical user in the new FSCM setup web service ( local shortcut) for the post processing.

 

Basis Technology NW ABAP User Administration and Authorization FIN (Finance)

entry.title: Webinar: Beyond Integration - How PepsiCo Builds Custom HR Applications with SAP BTP
entry.content:
Many organizations have a need to build custom HR applications and extensions to complement their SAP SuccessFactors solutions. As part of Comentec’s “SAP BTP for HR” webinar series, this episode moves beyond integration to explore the SAP Business Technology Platform's full potential for HR solutions.

Please join SAP BTP and SAP SuccessFactors partner, Comentec and PepsiCo's HR Technology team to demonstrate how SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) enables HR and HRIS to efficiently create high-quality custom HR extensions with limited development effort.

Using a Talent Card application as an example, Comentec will showcase how BTP's built-in templates, standards, and security features are combined to create robust solutions that HR technology teams can easily maintain while PepsiCo will share their firsthand experience of using BTP to develop a secure, sustainable HR application that utilizes data from SAP SuccessFactors.

When:  Date: April 2, 2025. |  Time: 11:30AM US EST, 17:30 CET
Registration Link

Join us to discover how SAP BTP's standardized components and best practices can help you:

  • Develop custom applications for SAP SuccessFactors with reduced effort and complexity
  • Create solutions that are easier to maintain and support internally
  • Leverage SAP's standard technology stack for improved sustainability
  • Build a stronger business case for custom application development

This session will help you reimagine the possibilities of BTP and provide you with the confidence to evaluate how it can be leveraged across your project roadmap.

This webinar is designed for:

  • Strategic decision-makers evaluating Employee Central and extensibility options
  • Technical leaders managing existing HR environment seeking modernization strategies
  • Business stakeholders wanting to improve data access, user experience, and HR processes.

Event info:
Date: April 2, 2025.
Time: 11:30AM US EST, 17:30 CET
Registration Link- this will take you to the registration page hosted by Comentec.

SAP Build Apps SAP Build Code SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: SAC optimized story - Issue with hirarchy objects
entry.content:
Hi all,

We encountered the following problem when working with SAC story in optimized mode. 

1. Created open ods view (facts) based on a database table (BW4HANA)

2. Created infoobject with hierarchy

3. Associated a field in open ods view with the hierarchy infoobject.

4. Created query on top of the open ods view.

5. Created live model in SAC based on the query we created earlier.

Now when we want to create a chart or table in SAC story optimized we are unable to add the hierarchy dimension. Everything freezes, we cant add any other dimension, we cant save the story, we cant change the data source, we cant delete the objects in story, we cant do anything.

But when we work in SAC story classic mode everything works fine. Looks like a bug to me. Any ideas?

Best regards, 

Martin Zluky

entry.title: Re: Priority for source of supply determination
entry.content:
What is your plan to use these vendors ?-- If you have two vendors in the source list without any indicator --the first line is selected by default --if you are planning to use a single vendor all the time --you can play with fix vendor indicator and MRP relevant indicator to drive the single vendor you want to use.

If you are expecting the system to switch vendors based on how much is ordered to them or allocate a % age --look to introduce the quota arrangement.

If you search SAP KBAs -How the source of supply assignment you will find the relevant KBAs on it. I would recommend making use of me.sap.com to look for KBAs.

entry.title: Re: SAP Private linky swear with Azure – running Cloud Connector and SAP Private Link side-by-side
entry.content:
Hi @Martin-Pankraz ,

 

Thanks for clarifying the above.

So which of the above scenario would I use to send messages from SAP ECC or S4 (both on Azure) to SAP CPI (again on Azure), without going over the public internet so that the traffic stays on the Azure backbone.

Is that Scenario 2 ?

Thanks

Saurabh

entry.title: Discontinued Materials Management
entry.content:
Introduction to Discontinued Materials

Discontinuation in SAP S/4HANA refers to the process of replacing obsolete components with follow-up materials in Material Requirements Planning (MRP). Once a material is marked as discontinued, dependent requirements are transferred to the follow-up material when stock is depleted or no longer available in sufficient quantities.

You can define discontinuation data in both the Material Master Record (MRP4 View) and the Bill of Materials (BOM). However, any follow-up item specified in the BOM takes priority over the follow-up material maintained in the material master.

Key Considerations for Discontinued Materials

System Behavior & Constraints

  • Multiple levels of discontinuation are not supported.

  • Once a material is assigned as a follow-up material, it cannot be discontinued further.

  • Discontinuation is not supported for co-products.

  • Once discontinuation data is maintained for an item, it cannot be assigned to an alternative item group.

Data Maintenance for Discontinued Material

Material Master – MRP4 View

  • Discontinuation Indicator: Marks the material as discontinued and enables MRP to transfer dependent requirements to a follow-up material.

  • Discontinuation Type:

    • Simple Discontinuation: A discontinued component is replaced with a follow-up material from a specific date.

    • Dependent Parallel Discontinuation: If one material (main component) is discontinued, a related subordinate component is also discontinued.

  • Follow-Up Material: The replacement material used in MRP once the original material is depleted.

BOM Discontinuation Data

  • The follow-up material in BOM takes precedence over the material master.

  • Discontinuation data must be explicitly maintained in BOM for proper material replacement during MRP.

Use of Discontinuation Data

Discontinuation data is displayed and entered in a dedicated dialog box. The following key elements are involved:

Discontinuation Indicator for Discontinued Material

  • If this indicator is set, the material is defined as 'to be discontinued', meaning MRP will transfer dependent requirements to the follow-up material when stock is depleted.

  • This indicator is maintained in the MRP data of the material master record and is displayed in BOM maintenance.

Discontinuation Groups

  • A freely-definable character string that groups together related discontinued items in a BOM.

  • The discontinued item is replaced by a follow-up item.

  • If implementing parallel discontinuation, you must define:

    • Material Master Record (MRP4 View):

      • Main material to be discontinued → Set Discontinuation Indicator = 1.

      • Subordinate material to be discontinued → Set Discontinuation Indicator = 3.

    • Bill of Material (BOM):

      • Assign the same discontinuation group to the main and subordinate items.

Follow-Up Groups

  • Another freely-definable character string that:

    • Groups together related follow-up items in a BOM.

    • Determines which discontinued items the follow-up item replaces.

    • The follow-up item replaces the discontinued item with the same discontinuation group value.

  • Entering a Follow-Up Group:

    • The follow-up group can only be entered if the item is kept in stock (e.g., stock item).

    • The follow-up item must be designated upon initial item entry and cannot be changed later.

  • Changing a Follow-Up Group:

    • If follow-up item data (such as quantity) is modified, SAP creates a new item record.

Prerequisites for Discontinuation

To ensure the correct functionality of discontinued materials, the following conditions must be met:

  1. Both discontinued material and follow-up material must be planned using MRP.

  2. The base unit of measure for the follow-up material must be the same as the discontinued material.

  3. The item category should be either a stock item or a variable-size item.

  4. Discontinuation is not supported for:

    • Document items

    • Text items

    • Class items

    • Intra materials

    • Alternative item groups

Steps to Maintain Discontinued Materials

Step 1: Update Material Master (MM02)

  • In the MRP4 View, for the discontinued material:

    • Set Discontinuation Indicator = 1.

    • Specify an Effective-Out Date.

    • Assign a Follow-Up Material.

Step 2: Update BOM (CS02)

  • Include the Follow-Up Material in the BOM to ensure replacement occurs correctly during planning.

Effect on MRP and Order Creation

Planning:

  • Dependent requirements are automatically assigned to the follow-up material once the effective-out date is reached and the original material stock is depleted.

Order Creation:

  • If the stock of the discontinued material is zero, the system will assign requirements directly to the follow-up material.

  • If discontinuation is set in the material master but not in BOM, MRP will not plan correctly, but the order will still show requirements for both materials based on BOM quantities.

  •  

Conclusion

By properly configuring discontinuation data in SAP S/4HANA, businesses can ensure a seamless transition from obsolete materials to follow-up components, maintaining efficiency in Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and production processes.

Reference

Discontinued Parts

entry.title: How to view item level data in Standard Fiori app (F2577)
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have a requirement from our business users that want to be able to see item level data underneath the header level data in the Track Sales Orders app (F2577). 

I figured a tree table would be their best option however I'm unsure if this can be achieved using controller extensions through BAS. I opened the project in the Adaptation Editor and it shows the current table being used is a smart table. I'm just looking for tips and advice on how we can achieve this.

entry.title: Re: SAP Private Link service use cases for SAP Cloud Integration and SAP Build Work Zone, Standard E
entry.content: There should be a disclaimer at the top of this blog to say that - "Although this is a SAP blog, SAP does not officially support the solution" (SAP has officially confirmed it on one of the incidents raised by us as it stands on 14-mar-2025)

entry.title: Failure Mode Group In Create Maintenance Request App
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm working in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition. In Asset Management, I would like to know if it is possible to select a different code group in Create Maintenance Request App, other than YB-PMGNL. I've created a new code group in Catalog D using the Maintain Code Groups App, but this doesn't appear in dropdown menu. So, Is it always necessary to enter a technical object in order to use different code groups?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Invoice Posting of Forwarding Agent against Shipment Cost Document
entry.content:
Very informative.

Thanks Neeraj for sharing detail document.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Invoice Posting of Forwarding Agent against Shipment Cost Document
entry.content: Informative content.

entry.title: Re: quality inspection process return the goods to the su...
entry.content:
When you reject the usage decision --the system proposes which bucket you want to move the stock -- if you enter qty in return posting -- the system will create a return posting --So for the future --double check where you posting in block or return posting to avoid manual return.

it seems you are putting your stock movement to block stock and causing this issue. You can move it to unrestricted and create a return delivery manually.

Manual return against original 101 documents--make sure you change the stock type from quality to block as your stock is set in block stock now.

entry.title: Re: Orchestrate your Generative AI workflows through the SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Hi Trinidad,  I'm dyin of embarrassment with u, but believe me, i've  searched high and low for references on how link a UDO with a Crystal Report. Seraching the sap blog, i saw a entry in which you participated a lot and yoy made reference to a blog where the code to be able to do what i require. Unfornately, that blog no longer exists and i have no way to link my report with my UDO. Finally, y managed to create a project ouside of SAP and call it form my UDO to print from another project, bu in that case, the report works, but only on my pc 😭. Do you think you can help me with references to be able to link my udo with my reportcorrectly and make it work form sap, or can you helo me with the code you used to achieve this?

Greetings from Guayaquil-Ecuador

entry.title: Re: Unlocking the Future of Sales & Service Business Processes with SAP Sales and Service Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @sophie_crepin,

Super cool!

However, there are no links to register, can you pls double-check the link settings for each of the webinar?

Looks like there is a type for Webinar 16, and 17...they both are scheduled for 14th of October 2025 @ 4pm CET. Is it just typo, can you pls check this?

Best Regards

Saurabh

entry.title: Re: Default G/L account during post goods movement 711 and 712
entry.content: Can you please screenshot of 711 and 712 from your system ?

entry.title: I need the y2 axis values to appear in my chart even if the y1 axis value = 0
entry.content:
I have a chart with ideally, my 8 groups appearing at all times, see below.  However, if one of the groups Total units = 0, the Adhoc and Units for 7 day supply (both static values) do not appear either and we need those no matter what.   It makes it obvious that a certain group has no inventory.   

In the attached, the 8th group is missing.

entry.title: Re: Hana Tools Issue in Eclipse
entry.content: If the Modeler perspective, Content, and Users folders are missing, go to Window > Perspective > Open Perspective > Other and select SAP HANA Modeler. Check Filters and Customization to ensure Catalog and Administration are enabled. Also, verify in Help > Installation Details that SAP HANA tools are installed. If issues persist, reinstall HANA tools.

entry.title: Re: Unable to connect to on-premise destination from BTP...
entry.content:
To access on-premise resources, I believe it's necessary to use the connectivity service as a proxy when making the call to the destination.

If the destination URL is used (without a proxy), it won't be possible to resolve the virtual host name defined in the Cloud Connector.

You may find these links helpful.

https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/consuming-connectivity-service?locale=en-US

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/connect-a-btp-application-to-on-premises-services/ba-p/13527922

Regards,

Toni

entry.title: Best patches for BI 4.3 SP04
entry.content: Could you please inform me which patches are best for BI 4.3 SP04? I'm looking for 300 or higer

entry.title: Re: Gl Account Hierachy DATA INSERTING TO THE NEW HIERACHRY
entry.content: To include the 10th GL account from 2024 in the 2025 hierarchy, you need I guess to update the hierarchy assignment. Please check transaction FSE2 (G/L Account Hierarchy) and manually add the missing GL account to the 2025 hierarchy. If using AFO or BW, you needs to ensure the hierarchy version update includes all required accounts.

entry.title: Re: Default G/L account during post goods movement 711 an...
entry.content: How you use 711-- it should be automatically determined -- for 711 and 712 -- Account Keys are BSX and GBB with General Modifier as INV-- so BSX debit or credit your Inventory account and GBB with INV will call offset account. It seems you have changed the standard configuration. As in SAP standard, system does not ask G/L account manually entered. It is based on Automatic Account determination concepts of valuation Group code, valuation area, valuation class, Chart of accounts, etc.

entry.title: Re: Does SAP BRF+ Comes under SAP HANA Clean core approac...
entry.content: SAP BRF+ aligns with the Clean Core approach because it allows business rules to be managed outside the core ERP system, reducing custom code modifications. It keeps the core standard, upgrade-safe, and flexible, as rules can be adjusted without changing the core application logic.

entry.title: Re: How to create user in a newly installed SAP CAL Appli...
entry.content:
In a newly installed SAP CAL S/4HANA 2023 appliance, log in with the default admin credentials provided in the SAP CAL instance details. If user creation is not visible, check transaction SU01 (for user management) or DBACOCKPIT for database-level access.
Also, verify system roles in SAP GUI or SAP Fiori. If login issues persist, please try to reset credentials in the SAP CAL console.

entry.title: Re: MDK: OpenDocument crashed when opening a pdf for the...
entry.content: just migrated to 24.11.3 which has been released today. and this issue is fixed. thank you Bill & team!
